daily_schedule.activity: Arrival at King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah. After completing immigration and baggage claim, you will be greeted by our representative and transferred directly to your 5-star hotel in Makkah, which is conveniently connected to the Haram. The rest of the day is dedicated to rest, freshening up, and performing Wudu. In the evening, a brief orientation session will be held in the hotel lobby to explain the upcoming Hajj rituals, daily schedules, and safety guidelines. Dinner will be served at the hotel. You may also visit the Haram for Isha prayer and spend time in individual worship and reflection.

Today you will perform your first Umrah before the official Hajj days begin. After Fajr prayer, you will put on Ihram from the hotel, then proceed to Masjid al-Haram. You will perform Tawaf (seven circuits around the Kaaba), followed by Sa’i between Safa and Marwah (seven laps). After completing Umrah, you can exit Ihram by trimming your hair. The rest of the day is free for personal worship, reading Quran, and making Dua. In the afternoon, our guide will explain the steps of Hajj in detail. Dinner will be served at the hotel. You may return to the Haram for night prayers.

This is a preparatory day before heading to Mina. After breakfast, our guide will conduct a detailed session on the rituals of Hajj, including what to expect on the Day of Arafah, how to perform Rami correctly, and the rules of staying in Mina and Muzdalifah. You will receive a Hajj kit containing a guidebook, prayer mat, and necessary supplies. In the afternoon, you should prepare your Ihram and pack a small bag for the tent days. After Maghrib prayer, a light dinner will be served. The rest of the evening is for rest, as tomorrow you will leave for Mina after Fajr.

Day of Arafah — the most important day of Hajj. After Fajr, you will board an air-conditioned bus to Mina, but you will not stay long — you will proceed directly to Arafat. Upon arrival at Arafat, you will spend the day standing in Wuquf from noon until sunset, engaged in Dua, Dhikr, and seeking forgiveness. This is the pinnacle of Hajj. Our guide will lead group Duas throughout the day. Meals and cold drinks will be provided at the tent. After sunset, you will move to Muzdalifah without praying Maghrib — you will combine Maghrib and Isha at Muzdalifah. You will collect 49 pebbles for Rami and spend the night under the open sky.

First day of Eid al-Adha. After performing Fajr in Muzdalifah, you will leave before sunrise to return to Mina. Today you will perform Rami al-Aqabah by throwing 7 pebbles at the largest Jamarah. After Rami, you will sacrifice an animal (Hady) — this is arranged by our package. Then you will shave or trim your hair (Halq or Taqsir). This allows you to exit the first stage of Ihram. You may then take a shower and change into regular clothes. After that, you will go to Makkah to perform Tawaf al-Ifadah and Sa’i. This completes the second stage. You will return to Mina to spend the night.

Second day of Tashreeq. After Dhuhr prayer, you will go to the Jamarat Bridge to perform Rami on all three pillars. First, throw 7 pebbles at the small Jamarah, then 7 at the middle Jamarah, and finally 7 at the large Jamarah (total 21 pebbles). Our guide will demonstrate the correct method and timing to avoid crowds. After Rami, you will return to your Mina tent for rest and meals. You may also visit other tents for social gatherings and Islamic lectures. In the evening, dinner will be served. You must spend another night in Mina as per the Hajj requirements.

Third day of Tashreeq. Today you will repeat the Rami ritual exactly as you did yesterday — 7 pebbles each at the small, middle, and large Jamarah (total 21 pebbles). If you are in a hurry to leave Mina, you may complete Rami after Dhuhr and then depart for Makkah. However, staying until Asr is recommended. After completing all Rami, you are completely free from Ihram restrictions. You will then take a bus back to your Makkah hotel. The rest of the day is for rest, shower, and laundry. In the evening, you may visit the Haram for Tawaf or simply pray there.

A well-deserved rest day after the intense Hajj rituals. Breakfast will be served late, and there is no mandatory activity scheduled. You may choose to sleep in, perform optional prayers at the Haram, or do Tawaf Nafl (voluntary circuits around the Kaaba). Our guide will be available in the lobby for any questions about incomplete rituals or missed steps. You can also use this day to do laundry, call family back home, or visit nearby shops for souvenirs and dates. Lunch and dinner will be served at the hotel. Please hydrate well as the weather can be dry.

Today is another free day for individual worship, but we have organized an optional group visit to the Kiswa Factory (where the Kaaba cover is made) for interested pilgrims. The visit lasts about two hours and includes a guided tour explaining the history and craftsmanship of the Kiswa. If you prefer not to join, you may spend the entire day at the Haram — reading Quran, making Dua, and performing Nafl Tawaf. Our guide will hold a Q&A session after Asr prayer at the hotel to clarify any remaining doubts about Hajj validity. Dinner will be served as usual.

Final Rami for those who stayed beyond the 12th of Dhul Hijjah — but in our schedule, today is a makeup day for anyone who missed any Rami on previous days. Our guide will accompany any pilgrim who needs to return to Jamarat Bridge. For others, this is a full day of prayer at the Haram. In the afternoon, we will conduct a Farewell Tawaf preparation session explaining the rules and importance of Tawaf al-Wada. You must perform Farewell Tawaf before leaving Makkah. The hotel will pack a small snack box for anyone performing late-night Tawaf.

After breakfast, you will check out of the Makkah hotel and board a luxury bus for Madinah. The journey takes approximately 5–6 hours, with one rest stop along the way. Upon arrival in Madinah, you will check into your hotel, which is located close to Masjid al-Nabawi. After settling in, you will visit the Prophet’s Mosque for the first time, offer Salah, and greet the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) from a distance if the Rawdah area is crowded. In the evening, dinner will be served at the hotel, followed by a short orientation about Madinah ziyarat.

Today is dedicated to visiting historical Islamic sites in Madinah. After Fajr, you will visit Masjid Quba — the first mosque built in Islam — and offer 2 Rak'ah Nafl prayer there. Next, you will go to Mount Uhud and visit the cemetery of the martyrs, including Hamza (RA). You will then visit Masjid al-Qiblatain (the mosque with two qiblas). After lunch, you will visit the Seven Mosques (Sab'a Masajid) and the Battle of the Trench site. Our guide will explain the historical significance of each location. You will return to the hotel before Asr for rest. Dinner at the hotel.

Today you will have the opportunity to perform Rawdah prayer — the garden from Paradise between the Prophet’s minbar and his grave. Our team will help you secure a permit through the Nusuk app. You must follow the designated time slot strictly. After performing Rawdah, you can spend the rest of the day in Masjid al-Nabawi, listening to lectures, reading Quran, and making Dua. In the afternoon, a group Dhikr session will be held at the hotel. Dinner will be served. You may also visit the Madinah date market in the evening to buy high-quality Ajwa dates.

This is your final full day in Madinah. After Fajr, you will visit the Madinah Qur’an Printing Complex, which prints millions of copies of the Quran distributed worldwide. The tour is educational and inspiring. After lunch, you will have free time for last-minute shopping at the Al Noor Mall or the old bazaar near the Prophet’s Mosque. You may also perform additional Nafl prayers at Masjid al-Nabawi. In the evening, a farewell dinner will be organized at the hotel, and our guide will provide departure instructions for the next day. Pack your bags carefully tonight.

After Fajr prayer, you will return to Masjid al-Nabawi one last time to offer a final farewell prayer. Then you will check out of the hotel and board the bus to King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah. The journey takes about 5 hours. Upon arrival at the airport, our representative will help you with check-in, baggage drop, and immigration. You will then proceed to the departure gate. May Allah accept your Hajj and Umrah. The flight departs in the evening, and meals will be served on board.

title: Economy Umrah Package - 14 Days

daily_schedule.activity: Arrival at King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah. After completing passport control and collecting your luggage, you will be greeted by our representative holding a sign with your name. You will then be transferred by a comfortable air-conditioned bus directly to your 3-star hotel in Makkah, located approximately 2 kilometers from Masjid al-Haram. The journey from Jeddah to Makkah takes about 1.5 hours. Upon arrival at the hotel, you will complete check-in formalities and receive your room keys. The rest of the day is free for rest and recovery from your flight. A light dinner will be delivered to your room. You may also walk to the Haram to perform Isha prayer if you feel energetic. Our guide will brief you tomorrow morning about Umrah performance.

Today is the day you have been waiting for — you will perform your first Umrah. After Fajr prayer at the hotel, you will put on your Ihram (men) or regular modest clothing (women) after performing Ghusl and making the intention. Our guide will walk with the group to Masjid al-Haram. Upon entering, you will perform Tawaf al-Qudum (arrival Tawaf) which consists of 7 circuits around the Kaaba, starting from the Black Stone corner. After completing Tawaf, you will pray 2 Rak'ah behind Maqam Ibrahim if possible. Then you will proceed to Safa and Marwah to perform Sa’i — walking 7 laps between the two hills. After completing Sa’i, men will shave or trim their hair, while women trim a fingertip's length. This completes your Umrah. You are now free from Ihram. Return to hotel for lunch and rest.

After a fulfilling day of Umrah yesterday, today is dedicated to rest and optional worship. You may sleep in and have breakfast at your convenience. There are no mandatory group activities scheduled. However, our guide will be available in the hotel lobby from 10 AM to 12 PM to answer any questions and to provide tips for performing additional voluntary Umrah if you wish. You may choose to go to the Haram on your own for Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha prayers. The atmosphere inside the Haram is deeply spiritual, and you can spend hours reading Quran, making Dua, and observing the beauty of the Kaaba. Lunch and dinner will be served at the hotel. Use this day to hydrate well and rest your feet.

Today we have organized a historical tour outside the Haram area. After breakfast, you will board a bus to visit the Mountain of Light (Jabal al-Noor), where the Cave of Hira is located — the place where the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) received the first revelation. You will not climb the mountain unless you are physically fit, but you can see it from the base and take photographs. Next, you will visit the Mountain of Mercy (Jabal al-Rahmah) in Arafat, followed by a visit to Muzdalifah and the Mina tent city. Our guide will explain the significance of each location for Hajj. You will return to the hotel by Asr time. The rest of the day is free for rest or Haram visit.

Today is another free day for personal worship and exploration. After breakfast, you may choose to perform a second voluntary Umrah. If you decide to do so, our guide can accompany a small group to the Haram and assist with the rituals. Alternatively, you may simply spend the entire day at the Haram — sitting near the Kaaba, making Dua, and reading Quran. Many pilgrims find that spending long hours in contemplation brings them closer to Allah. You can also explore the Abraj Al Bait shopping center adjacent to the Haram, where you can buy dates, perfumes, prayer rugs, and souvenirs. Lunch and dinner are at the hotel. Please remember to drink plenty of Zamzam water.

Today is your last full day in Makkah before traveling to Madinah tomorrow. After breakfast, you will perform a final Farewell Tawaf (Tawaf al-Wada) if you wish — though it is not mandatory for Umrah pilgrims, it is highly recommended. Our guide will lead a group to the Haram after Dhuhr prayer for this purpose. After completing Tawaf, you may spend the remaining hours in prayer. In the evening, a special group dinner will be held at the hotel where you can share experiences with fellow pilgrims. Our guide will provide instructions for tomorrow's departure to Madinah, including check-out time and baggage rules. Pack your bags tonight.

After Fajr prayer and breakfast, you will check out of your Makkah hotel. You will board a luxury bus for Madinah, which takes approximately 5 to 6 hours including one rest stop. Upon arrival in Madinah, you will check into your 3-star hotel located about 1.5 kilometers from Masjid al-Nabawi. After settling in and resting for an hour, you will walk to the Prophet's Mosque for the first time. You will offer Salutation to the Prophet (PBUH) and his two companions (Abu Bakr and Umar RA). You will also try to visit the Rawdah area if it is not too crowded. In the evening, dinner will be served at the hotel.

Today is dedicated to exploring the holy sites in Madinah. After Fajr prayer at Masjid al-Nabawi, you will visit Masjid Quba on foot or by bus — praying 2 Rak'ah there is equivalent to one Umrah in reward. Next, you will go to Mount Uhud, visit the cemetery of the martyrs, and pray for them. You will then visit Masjid al-Qiblatain, where the direction of prayer was changed from Jerusalem to Makkah. After lunch, you will visit the Seven Mosques complex and the Battle of the Trench site. Our guide will provide detailed historical commentary throughout the tour. You will return to the hotel by Asr. After Maghrib, you may return to the Prophet's Mosque for Isha.

Today you have the opportunity to perform Rawdah prayer inside Masjid al-Nabawi. Our team will help you secure a permit through the official Nusuk or Tawakkalna app. Rawdah is the area between the Prophet's minbar and his grave, described as a garden from Paradise. You must follow your allotted time slot strictly, usually lasting 10–15 minutes. After performing Rawdah, you can spend the rest of the day in the mosque, listening to Islamic lectures, reading Quran, and making Dua. In the afternoon, a group Dhikr session will be held at the hotel. Dinner will be served. You may also visit the Madinah dates market in the evening.

Today is a free day for individual worship and personal exploration in Madinah. After breakfast, you may choose to return to Masjid al-Nabawi on your own or with a small group. The mosque is open 24 hours, and you can pray at any time. You can also explore the streets around the mosque, which are filled with shops selling dates, prayer beads, abayas, thobes, and Islamic books. If you have not yet visited the Madinah Museum or the Hejaz Railway Museum, today is a good day to do so. Lunch and dinner will be served at the hotel. Take this day to rest and reflect on your spiritual journey so far.

Today you will return to Makkah after spending several beautiful days in Madinah. After Fajr prayer and breakfast, you will check out of your Madinah hotel and board the bus back to Makkah. The journey takes about 5–6 hours. Upon arrival, you will check back into your original hotel in Makkah (or a similar hotel if the previous one is fully booked). After resting and having lunch, you may go to Masjid al-Haram to perform Tawaf and offer prayers. This is also a good day to perform a voluntary Umrah if you have the energy and time. Dinner will be served at the hotel.

Today is your final opportunity to perform Umrah if you wish. After Fajr, our guide will lead a group to the Haram for a collective Umrah performance. The process is the same as before — Ihram, Tawaf, Sa’i, and Halq/Taqsir. This will be your last Umrah of this journey, so make sincere Duas. After completing Umrah, you will return to the hotel for lunch. The rest of the day is free for final prayers at the Haram. In the evening, a Farewell Tawaf (Tawaf al-Wada) will be organized for the entire group. This Tawaf is mandatory before leaving Makkah. After Tawaf, you will return to the hotel.

Today is your final full day in Saudi Arabia. After breakfast, you have free time for last-minute shopping, visiting the Haram for final prayers, or simply resting. You may also write letters or send messages to family back home sharing your experiences. Our guide will hold a short meeting after Dhuhr to confirm departure details for tomorrow, including bus departure time from the hotel, flight check-in procedures, and baggage weight limits. Please ensure your bags are packed and weighed tonight. A special farewell group dinner will be held at the hotel. After Isha, you may visit the Haram one last time.

After Fajr prayer, you will return to Masjid al-Haram for a final farewell prayer. Then you will return to the hotel for breakfast and check-out. You will board a bus to King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah, which takes about 1.5 hours. Upon arrival, our representative will assist with check-in, luggage drop, and immigration. You will then proceed to the departure gate. Reflect on your journey and make Dua for acceptance. The flight will depart in the afternoon or evening depending on your airline. In-flight meals will be served. May Allah accept your Umrah and bring you back again.

includes: 3-star hotel in Makkah (2 km from Haram)
3-star hotel in Madinah (1.5 km from Masjid Nabawi)
